heading: Concealing person from arrest
label: 1071 Concealing person from arrest
text: Whoever harbors or conceals any person for whose arrest a warrant or process has been issued under the provisions of any law of the United States, so as to prevent his discovery and arrest, after notice or knowledge of the fact that a warrant or process has been issued for the apprehension of such person, shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than one year, or both; except that if the warrant or process issued on a charge of felony, or after conviction of such person of any offense, the punishment shall be a fine under this title, or imprisonment for not more than five years, or both.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 246 (, ).Mar. 4, 1909, ch. 321, § 14135 Stat. 1114, U.S.C., 1940 ed., was divided. Part is in this section and the remainder is incorporated in .Section 246 of title 18section 752 of this titleMinor changes were made in phraseology.

Amendments2002— substituted “fine under this title” for “fine of under this title”.Pub. L. 107–2731994— substituted “under this title” for “not more than $1,000” after “person, shall be fined” and for “not more than $5,000” after “shall be a fine of”.Pub. L. 103–3221954—Act , increased the penalty from 6 months to 1 year where the violator harbored a person for whom process has been issued on a misdemeanor charge and inserted the penalty provision where the violation occurred after a person has been convicted of any offense or where a process has been issued for a felony.Aug. 20, 1954
